findings.detail: Clean horizontal LR pipeline at 1384x233. Five-step main path Query -> Embed -> Retrieve -> Inject -> Respond reads left-to-right with no node overlaps, no truncation, no edge crossings. Knowledge-base feeder enters Retrieve via a dotted edge with a legible 'chunks by meaning' label on white background, exactly matching the intent (single path + KB feeder). No agent/tool-use nodes present (4.4/4.5 drift avoided). Governing concept lives in the title field ('RAG -- fresh knowledge at query time, model unchanged'), not a node.

All six node labels (Query, Embed, Retrieve, Inject, Respond, Knowledge base) map 1:1 to the corpus's five-step RAG flow and Knowledge Base sections; subtitles ('closest chunks win', 'chunks + question in prompt', 'answer cites its source') paraphrase corpus language. No bare acronyms -- RAG appears only in the title where the corpus defines it as the topic headline. All labels <= 4 words.

SOFT, non-blocking: the KB feeder forces Retrieve/Inject/Respond onto a lower band than Query/Embed, producing a small dogleg and empty space top-right / bottom-left. Flow remains unambiguous and readable; acceptable as rendered. If revised later, anchoring KB and Q on the same rank would flatten the main path.

labels: (ghost) vs bare model: frozen memory only

preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels trace to corpus Core Concepts: five-step flow, knowledge base, bare-model contrast; no agents/tool-use scope creep)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in title: field; ghost node is a contrast annotation, not the displaced central concept)
preflight.single_path: pass (Q->E->R->I->A linear; KB feeder explicitly sanctioned by intent; ghost spur is annotation-only dotted link)
